title: calceolaria odontophylla
intro: Calceolaria odontophylla, also known as the 'dainty Nancy' or 'pocket flower', is a species of flowering plant in the family Calceolariaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it useful in treating respiratory issues and wounds. The health benefits of Calceolaria odontophylla include its ability to alleviate coughs, colds, and bronchitis, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Calceolaria odontophylla include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness the plant's medicinal properties.
